svg.text: Config File → Manager → Python venv → gRPC Stream from grpc-in → Script Execution
PHASE 1 — On-Prem Configuration Delivery (File-Based via Config Manager)
Note: On-prem JSON structure mirrors the Dapr FlowConfig but is delivered as a file, NOT via Kafka.
PHASE 2 — Virtual Environment & Script Setup (Identical to Cloud Mode)
Same process as C2C: CreateVirtualEnv → Base64 Decode Scripts → Template(infoblox_driver.py.tmpl) → Execute
Key difference: --cloud-mode false (no --kafka-consumer, no --account-id). Instead: --addr pointing to local grpc-in service on port 9195.
Venv created at: /src/python/venv/venv-{flowId}/ — one per flow, shared across data types of the same flow
Receives Cloud data → Writes to on-prem topics
Also receives Dapr PubSub events from cloud Kafka
for resp in client_stream: # ClientCtrl_Messages from grpc-in
On-Prem Topic Naming (NOT Kafka — internal gRPC topics):
• NO Kafka involved — grpc-in acts as the message broker
• NO account_id prefix — only flow_id + data_type
• Data comes from grpc-in's internal cache (parsed Parquet/CSV data)
How grpc-in Gets Data for SOAR-Light (On-Prem Data Path)
• grpc-in receives data from BOTH Cloud (via grpc-out pulling from data.exporter.kafka) AND NIOS (via dns-in/rpz-in/flume)
• grpc-in parses incoming data (Parquet/CSV) into events and caches them indexed by {flow_id}_{data_type} topic
• soar-light's Python process connects to grpc-in:9195 as a gRPC client and subscribes to specific topics
• grpc-in streams events to soar-light in real-time — soar-light acknowledges with MarkMessages after processing
• grpc-in effectively acts as the on-prem "message broker" replacing Kafka in the cloud deployment model
